title: I tested the Ninja Creami against five popular competitors, and the results shocked me
summary: The Ninja Creami is one of the best ice cream makers, but are there better options? I tested it against KitchenAid, Cuisinart Fast Freeze and more to find out.
story: In 2023, my colleague and NBC Select reporter Zoe Malin tested and wrote a review of the Ninja Cream. This was back when it first launched and the appliance was selling out every other day and even your local Costco or Best Buy couldn’t keep up with the demand. Today, thankfully the beloved appliance is much easier to get a hold of — in fact, the brand has since launched a deluxe version and multiple frozen treat and beverage makers, including the Ninja Slushi and Ninja Creami Soft Serve Maker.

More than two years later, the original Ninja Creami is still popular and has developed quite a following. I keep up with a lot of food influencers on social media, and I’m served at least a few Ninja Creami recipe ideas a month. But I’m also aware that many competitors have launched ice cream makers too, many of which come at lower price points. And while $229 isn’t insanely expensive for a small home appliance from a premium brand like Ninja, there are similar products that are at least $30 to $50 cheaper, from brands like Cuisinart, KitchenAid and Nutribullet.

Additionally, some competitors have recently launched similar frozen treat makers that, while more expensive, have features that the Ninja Creami doesn’t, such as the GreenPan Frost, which doesn’t require overnight freezing.

So, Malin and I spent a few weeks testing the following appliances to see how they performed compared to the Ninja Creami: Cuisinart Fast Freeze, Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker, KitchenAid Ice Cream Maker Attachment, Nutribullet Chill and the GreenPan Frost. Here’s what we uncovered.

Want more from NBC Select? Sign up for our newsletter, The Selection, and shop smarter.
Ninja Creami versus Cuisinart, KitchenAid, Nutribullet and GreenPan

Below are descriptions of our experience testing the ice cream makers from popular kitchen brands and comparing them to the Ninja Creami. Aside from being common competitors for the Ninja Creami, many of these ice cream makers have at least a four-star average rating from 500 or more reviews on Amazon.
Ninja Creami NC301 Ice Cream Maker
Easy to set upMany pre-set recipesWorks fast after freezing
Easy to set up
Many pre-set recipes
Works fast after freezing
It’s loud
It’s loud

Ninja sent me the Creami to test for this story, which I chose over the Deluxe since it has more reviews overall. This was the first appliance that I tested and I used the ice cream recipe that comes in the included recipe booklet, which has a cream cheese base (instead of the common egg base for traditional ice cream). Aside from that, I did a simple vanilla ice cream recipe with heavy cream, sugar, whole milk and vanilla extract. Like with most at-home ice cream makers, the Ninja Creami requires you to freeze the ice cream base at least 24 hours ahead of time to ensure that it reaches the right texture when you actually make it. It also comes with pints and a mixing bowl with a handle, both of which are dishwasher safe.

After testing it myself, sharing some of my creations with my coworkers and comparing it to the appliances below, I can confirm that the Ninja Creami really does live up to the hype and it is definitely a 9.9 out of 10 for me (I took points off for loudness). It was easy to set up and wash all of the pieces ahead of time, thanks to the quick-start guide and, after freezing it for a day, the ice cream was ready in around 10 minutes.

The texture of the ice cream was creamy and smooth, even with an egg-free, cream cheese base that doesn’t require any tempering (increasing the temperature of beaten eggs and then adding them to the warm ice cream base). It was also one of only two machines that I found had the closest texture to store-bought ice cream. It’s very loud, though, comparable to a blender or vacuum. But that shouldn’t be too much of a problem if you don’t have to make ice cream when people around your home are sleeping.
Cuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker
Small sizeEasy to storeVery lightweight
Small size
Easy to store
Very lightweight
Requires the most manual labor
Requires the most manual labor

Cuisinart sent me this appliance, which recently launched, and I was excited about it because I was already planning on testing the brand’s popular traditional ice cream maker, which has a different setup. This is much more lightweight and more compact compared to every other ice cream maker I tested.

While this ice cream maker has multiple pre-sets similar to the Ninja Creami and GreenPan Frost (reviewed below), it has a somewhat different process for making ice cream. While you still have to freeze the base ahead of time (I used the same cream cheese base recipe without any tempering or heat), this one has a handle on the top that you push up and down slowly as the machine runs. It takes more manual labor than any other machine I tested.

My colleagues and I were surprised by the results in the best way — it was almost identical in texture, taste and creaminess to the Ninja Creami. It was much smoother than I expected, since it didn’t have any eggs, and it didn’t have an icy texture that sometimes happens with homemade ice cream recipes. Some of my coworkers actually preferred this one over the Ninja Creami, though I believe they were both delicious, with this one being a very close second place for me. Aside from the fact that the ice cream came out great, it also was made in a small and lightweight appliance that is less than half the price of the Ninja Creami.
While I don’t think this is better than the Creami, per se, I do think it’s a great alternative for someone who wants a compact appliance, like apartment dwellers and those low on space.
KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment
Easy to installEasy to storeLightweight
Easy to install
Easy to store
Lightweight
Requires freezing for 24 hours
Requires freezing for 24 hours

KitchenAid sent Malin this ice cream maker attachment to test, courtesy of the brand. As an avid baker, she uses her KitchenAid Stand Mixer often, so setting up the ice cream maker bowl was simple. Unlike the Ninja Creami, Cuisinart FastFreeze and the GreenPan Frost, this ice cream maker is just an insulated bowl with non-toxic freezing liquid that helps keep the bowl and ice cream cold when it’s mixing.

Malin made the ice cream base a day ahead before installing the bowl to the mixer and says it was easy and fast to do. “I love making homemade ice cream, but I can’t fit another appliance in my tiny kitchen, so being able to add this attachment to my KitchenAid and turn it into an ice cream maker solves my problem,” she says.

The biggest drawback to this pick is the obvious: It’s an attachment to the KitchenAid mixer, so if you don’t already have one, you can’t use it. We did think it was worth testing since the stand mixer is so popular; anyone considering putting this on their wedding registry, housewarming wishlist, or just considering splurging on it for the summer should know what they’re getting.

Overall, this was my third place choice for all the ice cream makers. The texture was great but it doesn’t have as many pre-sets as the Creami or FastFreeze and it requires a separate appliance. It’s not better than the Ninja Creami, but it’s perfect for anyone who already has a Kitchenaid Stand Mixer and wants to make their own ice cream.
Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker
Simple setupMakes a large portionAffordable
Simple setup
Makes a large portion
Affordable
Doesn’t have pre-sets
Doesn’t have pre-sets

I made ice cream with this ice cream maker a year or so ago, but the brand sent me a courtesy one to refresh my memory. Similar to the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, this style of ice cream maker is much more simple in design and doesn’t have pre-sets for soft-serve, sorbet or similar, just traditional ice cream. The appliance includes an insulated bowl lined with a freezing liquid that you have to freeze a day in advance, a base that rotates the bowl with the ice cream base, a paddle and a cover.

I’ve made both traditional ice cream with a tempered egg base and a version with a cream cheese base, and overall, while both were delicious, I found that this appliance is best for traditional ice cream, which has eggs in it. The freezing method and the paddle help the ice cream achieve a great creamy texture that isn’t icy and is easy to scoop. Compared to the other ice cream makers on this list, the paddle is goes further into the freezer base, which I believe helps make the texture creamier.

After freezing the bowl for a day and then preparing the base, it took about 20 minutes for the ice cream to start forming. Overall, I believe this is the easiest of all the appliances to use, so it’s great for anyone who usually bypasses most of the instructions since it’s quite intuitive to use.
Compared to the Creami, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or customizations for texture, but it makes for the best egg-based ice cream.
GreenPan Frost Ice Cream & Frozen Drink Maker
Doesn’t require freezingMakes a large portionEasy to clean
Doesn’t require freezing
Makes a large portion
Easy to clean
It’s heavy
It’s heavy

This is one of the newest appliances on this list, so I was excited to try out a courtesy sample, which the brand sent me. I love GreenPan because of its non-toxic cookware, including its popular Stanley Tucci collection, which I’ve had for years and has held up very well. This appliance makes soft service ice cream, sorbet, milkshakes and other frozen drinks, like margaritas or frosés.

I tested the ice cream function of this machine and it didn’t take long to set up or start using because it doesn’t require freezing in advance. This ice cream maker has a built-in feature that uses electricity to freeze the ingredients as it turns in the bowl. This is easily the best feature of the appliance if you prefer soft serve over traditional ice cream, and I also love the old-fashioned style of the lever on the front.

What I also love about it is that it has a dial on the front that, after selecting the pre-set you want, you can use to customize the texture. Additionally, while the freezer bowls for some of the ice cream makers above aren’t dishwasher safe, the parts on this machine are not only dishwasher safe, but it also has a feature that removes excess residue while you add soap and water to it as an alternative way to clean it. This was easy to do and didn’t require me to use a sponge or anything.

Compared to the Ninja Creami, the Frost makes much softer ice cream, so it’s not necessarily better, but it’s best for people who love soft serve or fro-yo. With that said, I love that it doesn’t require any freezing ahead of time, which all the others do.
Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker
Small sizeEasy to useHas multiple pre-sets
Small size
Easy to use
Has multiple pre-sets
Best for fruit-based mixes
Best for fruit-based mixes

While I love my Nutribullet personal blender, which has helped me make amazing salsas, smoothies and protein shakes, this was my first introduction to their recently launched frozen treat appliance. The brand sent Malin a courtesy sample of the ice cream maker to test for this story, and she made two types of bases: a dairy-based vanilla ice cream mixture and a mixture made with frozen mango.

The machine works similarly to the personal blenders: It includes two 16-ounce cups with lids that you invert onto the base with the mixing blade. When you turn on the machine, the blade moves up and down to mix the ice cream or sorbet base, but this is more automatic than manual. After testing both the ice cream and sorbet mixture, Malin and I both felt like the mango-based sorbet turned out the best texture-wise, while the ice cream, while delicious, was very soft in texture, similar to soft serve, which I don’t mind (though I like regular ice cream more).

“It’s a little cheaper than the Creami and it’s more compact in size, more lightweight and quieter than the Creami,” says Malin. The sorbet came out great in the Chill but the ice cream was much softer compared to the Creami, so it’s only better if you prefer sorbet or fruit-based frozen desserts.
How I tested the ice cream makers against the Ninja Creami
There are a few key considerations that went into testing and the criteria I used to judge each of the ice cream makers:

Setup: As with any kitchen appliance, the first thing that you consider is how easy (or intimidating) it is to follow the directions on the instruction manual to make sure you use it correctly without breaking it or getting bad results. Luckily, all of the ice cream makers above were relatively easy to set up. Outside of rinsing the removable parts before the first use, there wasn’t much else to do aside from preparing the base in advance (for all of them except the GreenPan Frost). The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker was the easiest overall — it literally only has an on/off button on the front and that’s all you have to know after freezing the bowl ahead of time. While the GreenPan Frost doesn’t require freezing in advance, it does have slightly more parts to clean before first use compared to the others. Ease of use: Similar to setup, all of the ice cream makers were easy to use after a few reads of the instruction manual, but the appliances I found to be the most streamlined were the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker. The Ninja Creami’s quick-start guide is very helpful in understanding how to use it the first time and in addition to having many pre-sets, it also has a re-spin function that lets you add more milk or liquid while mixing to help you achieve the texture you prefer, which I loved. While the Cuisinart FastFreeze came out with one of the best textures, it does require you to push the handle down slightly to mix the ice cream, so I’d get this for someone young moving into their first (small) apartment instead of an elderly relative. Ice cream texture: The most important factor in all of this testing is how the actual ice cream came out. I wanted to see which ice cream maker made something that felt the most similar to traditional egg-based ice cream and/or which one had the creamiest texture that didn’t have tiny unblended pieces of ice or graininess. Overall, all of the ice cream makers delivered delicious flavor, though some of the textures, such as from the GreenPan and Nutribullet, were much more similar to soft serve (the GreenPan one makes it clear that it’s best for this style) while the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart FastFreeze had the best texture to me, though the ice cream from the original Cuisinart was also great.

Setup: As with any kitchen appliance, the first thing that you consider is how easy (or intimidating) it is to follow the directions on the instruction manual to make sure you use it correctly without breaking it or getting bad results. Luckily, all of the ice cream makers above were relatively easy to set up. Outside of rinsing the removable parts before the first use, there wasn’t much else to do aside from preparing the base in advance (for all of them except the GreenPan Frost). The Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker was the easiest overall — it literally only has an on/off button on the front and that’s all you have to know after freezing the bowl ahead of time. While the GreenPan Frost doesn’t require freezing in advance, it does have slightly more parts to clean before first use compared to the others.

Ease of use: Similar to setup, all of the ice cream makers were easy to use after a few reads of the instruction manual, but the appliances I found to be the most streamlined were the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker. The Ninja Creami’s quick-start guide is very helpful in understanding how to use it the first time and in addition to having many pre-sets, it also has a re-spin function that lets you add more milk or liquid while mixing to help you achieve the texture you prefer, which I loved. While the Cuisinart FastFreeze came out with one of the best textures, it does require you to push the handle down slightly to mix the ice cream, so I’d get this for someone young moving into their first (small) apartment instead of an elderly relative.

Ice cream texture: The most important factor in all of this testing is how the actual ice cream came out. I wanted to see which ice cream maker made something that felt the most similar to traditional egg-based ice cream and/or which one had the creamiest texture that didn’t have tiny unblended pieces of ice or graininess. Overall, all of the ice cream makers delivered delicious flavor, though some of the textures, such as from the GreenPan and Nutribullet, were much more similar to soft serve (the GreenPan one makes it clear that it’s best for this style) while the Ninja Creami and the Cuisinart FastFreeze had the best texture to me, though the ice cream from the original Cuisinart was also great.
How to decide which ice cream maker is best for you
To decide which of the above ice cream makers is best for you, here is a brief breakdown of what I believe is each appliances’ best attributes, plus some drawbacks to keep in mind.

Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker: This appliance is highly versatile and customizable and creates great, creamy texture. It also comes with a helpful recipe guide. That said, it is the loudest of all the appliances, so be sure your family members and neighbors don’t mind. Cuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker: This is the smallest of all the appliances I tested and would be great for someone with a small apartment, and it has multiple pre-sets. This ice cream maker, however, requires manual effort, so this is great for someone fresh out of college or simply willing to do the arm workout in exchange for the lower price. KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment: If you’re an avid home baker and enjoy making ice cream every once in a while, this is the best option for you. I also think that since it works with a versatile appliance like the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, it’s also great for small apartments, though this means you must own a KitchenAid Stand Mixer. Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker: This is the best option for anyone who loves ice cream just the way it is, wants it to be the most similar to a store-bought tub, and doesn’t plan on making many custom mixes. I also believe this is the best option for people who aren’t active or skilled in the kitchen since it’s so simple to use. With that said, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or ways to customize the texture. GreenPan Frost Ice Cream Maker & Slushie Machine: If you and your family prefer soft serve ice cream or frozen yogurt over traditional ice cream, this is the best option for you. It’s also great for anyone who loves frozen beverages like slushies, milkshakes and margaritas. Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker: This is a great option for people who prefer to make soft sorbets or fruit-based frozen treats like açai bowls or sherbet. It’s also compact and great for small apartments, similar to the brand’s personal blenders.

Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker: This appliance is highly versatile and customizable and creates great, creamy texture. It also comes with a helpful recipe guide. That said, it is the loudest of all the appliances, so be sure your family members and neighbors don’t mind.

Cuisinart FastFreeze Ice Cream Maker: This is the smallest of all the appliances I tested and would be great for someone with a small apartment, and it has multiple pre-sets. This ice cream maker, however, requires manual effort, so this is great for someone fresh out of college or simply willing to do the arm workout in exchange for the lower price.

KitchenAid Cream Maker Attachment: If you’re an avid home baker and enjoy making ice cream every once in a while, this is the best option for you. I also think that since it works with a versatile appliance like the KitchenAid Stand Mixer, it’s also great for small apartments, though this means you must own a KitchenAid Stand Mixer.

Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker: This is the best option for anyone who loves ice cream just the way it is, wants it to be the most similar to a store-bought tub, and doesn’t plan on making many custom mixes. I also believe this is the best option for people who aren’t active or skilled in the kitchen since it’s so simple to use. With that said, it doesn’t have any pre-sets or ways to customize the texture.

GreenPan Frost Ice Cream Maker & Slushie Machine: If you and your family prefer soft serve ice cream or frozen yogurt over traditional ice cream, this is the best option for you. It’s also great for anyone who loves frozen beverages like slushies, milkshakes and margaritas.

Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker: This is a great option for people who prefer to make soft sorbets or fruit-based frozen treats like açai bowls or sherbet. It’s also compact and great for small apartments, similar to the brand’s personal blenders.

Overall, after trying all of the ice cream makers on this list, I enjoyed the Ninja Creami the best because the ice cream itself came out great — the texture was perfect and even though it requires freezing ahead of time, it mixes the ice cream up very fast. It also has a re-spin feature that allows you to adjust the texture of the ice cream and re-mix it. Plus, it has many pre-sets for gelato and sorbet.

A very close second for me was the Cuisinart FastFreeze, which is less expensive than the Ninja Creami and the ice cream had an almost identical texture. Plus, it has multiple pre-sets and is much more compact, which I loved.
Frequently asked questions

There are alternatives to the Ninja Creami that are both cheaper and more expensive, but whether or not they are “better,” depends on your preferences. If you want an ice cream maker that’s small, a great alternative would be the Cuisinart FastFreeze. If you prefer soft serve ice cream and don’t like having to freeze ice cream mixes ahead of time, then the GreenPan Frost is a suitable option. If you want the most traditional, store-bought style ice cream and don’t mind prepping food ahead of time, the Cuisinart Ice Cream Maker is a fit. For people who prefer sorbet over ice cream, the Nutribullet Chill Ice Cream Maker is an excellent choice.
Why trust NBC Select?

I’m a commerce editor at NBC Select, where I cover home and kitchen appliances, cleaning, organization and more. I’ve written stories about air fryers, coffee makers and vacuums, as well as shower cleaning products and more. I’ve also written about food and kitchen appliances for more than eight years and have previously worked as a food editor at multiple food magazines. For this story, I tested courtesy ice cream makers from several popular brands, with help from my colleague Zoe Malin (she tested two), who also writes about kitchen appliances for NBC Select.

title: Democrat Johnny Garcia wins Texas House primary after rival’s antisemitic comments
summary: Democrat Johnny Garcia has won his party’s primary in Texas’ 35th Congressional District, NBC News projects, defeating a rival whom party leaders had condemned for antisemitic comments as Democrats look to compete in a district Republicans redrew to their benefit
story: Democrat Johnny Garcia has won his party’s primary in Texas’ 35th Congressional District, NBC News projects, defeating a rival whom party leaders had condemned for antisemitic comments as Democrats look to compete in a district Republicans redrew to their benefit.
Subscribe to read this story ad-free
Get unlimited access to ad-free articles and exclusive content.

The district stretches from Austin to San Antonio, the result of Republican efforts to combine two Democratic seats into one and create a new district leaning their way. Donald Trump carried the district by about 10.5 points in 2024.

Despite that result, there are signs the district could be competitive in the general election, including $1 million in spending from an opaque outside group aimed at boosting Garcia’s opponent, sex therapist Maureen Galindo, despite the controversies dogging her. Punchbowl News reported that the super PAC, Lead Left PAC, had links to a GOP fundraising platform.

One TV ad from Lead Left framed Galindo as a fresh face compared with “weak Democrats,” highlighting her calls to eliminate U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement. Meanwhile, in a recent Instagram post, she wrote that she wanted to turn an ICE detention center “into a prison for American Zionists and former ICE officers for human trafficking,” among other things.

House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries of New York and a slew of other national Democratic leaders disavowed Galindo’s “antisemitic” and “dangerous” statements and castigated Republicans, whom they accused of being behind the effort to boost her.

Garcia will face Air Force veteran Carlos De La Cruz in the fall after he won the Republican primary runoff Tuesday night. Trump endorsed De La Cruz over state Rep. John Lujan, who had Gov. Greg Abbott’s backing.
De La Cruz is the brother of Rep. Monica De La Cruz, who represents a nearby district.
The big spending to boost Galindo suggests an implicit acknowledgment that the district, drawn by Republicans in the hopes of flipping it this fall, could be competitive unless she won the primary.

Brandon Steinhauser, a longtime Texas Republican strategist who previously was a top aide to Sen. John Cornyn, told NBC News that he has long thought Republican lawmakers’ decision to draw the congressional map the way they did was “making some big assumptions that President Trump’s support among Hispanics in Texas would translate in the future” to the rest of the party.

The new district’s voting-age population is about 52% Hispanic, according to data from the Texas Legislative Council. Despite Trump’s margin in the district in 2024, it has been more competitive in recent elections: Trump beat President Joe Biden by about 2 points there in 2020. In 2018, Democratic Rep. Beto O’Rourke and Republican Sen. Ted Cruz fought to a near-draw in the territory.

Meanwhile, since 2024, “polling has shown [Trump]’s probably lost a bit of that support both nationwide and in Texas,” Steinhauser said about Trump’s standing with Hispanic voters. “It’s probably fair to look at this and say it was a little aggressive or overconfident. It may not be, but the concerns could be real that doing it this way may actually not work out as well as they hoped.”

Steinhauser added that he doesn’t think that assumption should cost Republicans the seat. And he said the spending for Galindo was most likely partly an attempt to “invest now” in hope of being able to prevent an expensive general election, as well as an attempt to elevate Galindo and “tie a narrative together to say that Democrats are the party of antisemitism and radical politics.”

Meanwhile, the political committee aligned with the Blue Dog Democrats, which backs moderates in the party, spent about $700,000 on ads to boost Garcia. Another group, called Project 2018, spent a similar amount, while the Democratic Majority for Israel spent about $100,000.

Galindo’s presence in the runoff was a surprise: She finished first in the March 3 Democratic primary with 29% support despite having raised less than $10,000 for her bid. Both Galindo and Garcia finished well below the 50% threshold to win the primary outright, triggering a runoff under Texas law.

Republicans say the new congressional map can net them five more congressional seats from the state. Two of the new districts, in the Houston and Fort Worth areas, shouldn’t be problems given their heavy Republican leans. The two other districts along the Rio Grande Valley, currently held by Democrats, were made more Republican-leaning but will feature hard-fought battleground races in the fall.

It isn’t the first time Texas Democrats’ preferred candidate was pulled into a runoff against a controversial candidate in a key race. The front-runner for the Senate nomination in 2012 had to overcome a runoff against an activist who supported impeaching President Barack Obama and campaigned with a picture of Obama sporting a Hitler mustache. And as Republican-aligned groups appear to be boosting candidates in a handful of competitive Democratic primaries across the country, Democrats were doing the same four years ago to boost candidates they thought they could beat in key general elections.

Also Tuesday, three Republicans won primary runoffs in House seats their party is heavily favored to hold this fall. Two Trump-backed candidates, military veteran Alex Mealer in the 9th District and real estate professional Jon Bonck in the 38th District, secured their party’s nominations, as did former congressional aide Tom Sell in the 19th District.

title: Everything Ciara, West and Amanda Say In ‘Summer House’ Reunion Part 1
summary: Here’s what Ciara Miller, West Wilson and Amanda Batula said to each other in Part 1 of the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.
story: Bravo has aired Part 1 of the anticipated three-part “Summer House” Season 10 reunion, and Ciara Miller didn’t hold back when it came to how betrayed she feels by her co-stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson.

The explosive reunion was filmed April 23, less than a month after Amanda and West confirmed their relationship. This was a scandal that crippled the Bravoverse for its many layers: West is Ciara’s ex and Amanda is Ciara’s close friend; Amanda and her husband, fellow “Summer House” cast member Kyle Cooke, had just announced their split in January; viewers had also been watching West and Ciara rekindle their romance in new episodes of Season 10, which taped last summer.
Amanda and West explained in their joint public statement that their romantic “connection” was born out of a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”

“Summer House” cast members had since publicly shared their emotions about the scandal and what they did and didn’t know. Ciara gave her reaction in an interview with Glamour magazine, calling the news a “major mindf---.” West had spoken briefly in various episodes of his podcast, the most recent of which he said he and Kyle had three-hour conversation.
Amanda apologized in a statement shared to her Instagram story on April 10 without naming anyone specifically.
“I’m truly sorry to everyone I’ve disappointed and hurt, especially those I know personally who I’ve reached out to individually,” Amanda wrote.
She said she would be “addressing any and all questions honestly and directly” at the reunion.
Viewers had to patiently wait until May 26 to see how it all played out. Here’s what Amanda, West and Ciara had to say in Part 1 of the “Summer House” Season 10 reunion.
What Ciara Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion

A big chunk of this episode showed the “Summer House” cast preparing to take the reunion stage. Ciara was the first to receive a visit from host Andy Cohen, who had been making his rounds in the dressing areas.
She was visibly upset when she told Andy that Amanda was the person she felt most betrayed by. Her most pressing question for both Amanda and West was, “Why?”
When the cast took the reunion stage, Andy wasted no time addressing the scandal and asked Ciara when she suspected something was going on between Amanda and West.
Ciara gave a date, Jan. 17, when she texted Amanda to invite her to drinks with castmate Mia Calabrese and saw her phone’s location was at West’s house.
“I said, ‘Hm, that’s weird, why would she be at West’s house?’” Ciara said.
“Because West was there for me all summer,” Amanda said.
“Where was I?” Ciara said.
“Also there for me,” Amanda said.
“How many times have I asked you to hang out? Have I asked to come over to your house? Have I invited you to do stuff? And you act like your phone doesn’t work,” Ciara said.
Ciara clapped back at Amanda’s notion that West was fair to pursue.
“There are a million other f----ing guys in New York City, but you chose the one — and you know how much that would f--- me up — you chose the one guy,” she said.

“You did it on purpose because you’re a f----ing snake, Amanda. You’re a snake in the f----ing grass, is what you are. You’re a snake in the f----ing grass, and you should honestly just say OK because you know you are. You move silent, but you’re f----ing deadly.”

Ciara pulled out her phone to show texts that Amanda sent to Ciara the day that rumors first emerged online about Amanda and West. (These rumors circulated a few weeks before Amanda and West confirmed their relationship.)

Amanda’s text to Ciara said: “Hi, checking in. The internet is f----ing on one right now and I feel like everything I do is under a microscope and I can’t believe I’m even having to text you this right now but West and I are very much so just friends. I’m sad we’re even trying to discuss this.”
After Ciara read this text, Andy asked Amanda to clarify if she and West had been hooking up at that time. Amanda replied they had been “talking” and “everything that was happening” was “PG.”
Ciara read aloud her text back to Amanda: “So I can expect for there to be no photos or videos coming out? Feels like it’s such a widespread rumor for there to be no veracity to it.”

Amanda texted back: “Nothing different than what I’ve posted myself. People have just seen us with each other more and are being psychos. I hear what you’re saying, but I feel like I can’t even exist right now without people over analyzing everything I do.”
Ciara then texted back: “OK, so for clarity, since I don’t really feel like you’re making it clear, you and West haven’t hooked up, made out, held hands under their table, or had sex?”
Amanda then texted: “Correct.”
On the reunion stage, Amanda admitted, “I lied. I 100% lied.”

Ciara then read another text from Amanda that came a few days later: “Do you want to talk at all? I’m super in my head about where we left off this conversation, and I hate the thought of things being weird between us, because of Wes, like that’s so silly to me.”
When cast member Lindsay Hubbard suggested that Amanda and West will not end up together, Ciara countered that Amanda is the “best” type of woman for West.

“Someone who’s not going to check him on anything. That’s totally Amanda. She’s very mute. She’s going to be that weak figure that he needs, and he can always be the star in the relationship.” she said. “I actually think maybe it could work.”
Kyle then said to Ciara, “That was a little harsh.”
“That’s your wife. She’s not my wife,” Ciara replied.
What West Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion
At the reunion’s start, West told the group he wanted to clear up the “wildfire of what this became.”
Ciara accused West of lying about being in another relationship earlier this year.

“I think you’re the most fraudulent person sitting on this couch,” she told West. “We’ve been here three years now, and I don’t think anybody really truly knows who you are. You live a different life off camera than you do on camera. We don’t know what you’re doing, and probably the reason why it was so confusing is because you have a girlfriend: Meija.”
West said she was “not a girlfriend” but someone he was “seeing.”  Meija made him the horse-hair tie that West wore on “Watch What Happens Live” in March, he said.
Amanda said that she knew West was dating Meija at the time.
Cast member Jesse Solomon, who is West’s close friend, called both West and Amanda out for lying to him about their relationship when they had opportunities to tell the truth.

“I spoke with Amanda, I sat down with her for drinks, and she told me that it just wasn’t true,” Jesse said. “West, I don’t know, but I would text him asking, and he would tell me that it wasn’t true.”
West replied by owning up to lying.

“The lying part, I will not sit here and act like I didn’t do it,” he said. “I 100% lied to people here, and I take full accountability for that. It’s not cool to do in general, it’s not cool to do to your friends, and no matter the case, lying to people you care about is not OK.”
When Andy asked West and Amanda who made the first move, West said he voiced his feelings first.
“I kind of looked at Amanda, and I was like, ‘Am I my crazy, or is there a little something going on here?’”
He then said their first kiss was in February after a brunch. When asked when they first slept together, Amanda replied that it was after they released their public statement.
“Also to clear up — this isn’t a sex scandal. This was, like, hanging out,” West said.
Andy and multiple cast members asked West to address his mixed signals toward Ciara last summer, as seen on the show.

“I wouldn’t lie about a relationship being great, but I also recognized in that time I was not going to be what Ciara needed, and we ended it,” he said. “We got dinner right after summer, and I told Ciara what my plans were, and how much I learned the first time from what affected Ciara, and that I wasn’t going to just jump down that rabbit hole again.

I know a lot of things that can be casual for me are not casual for Ciara, and we had that conversation. And I’ve made plenty of mistakes along the way, but I was very proactive in trying to protect our friendship and not send wrong messages.”
Mia then asked West if he thought an emotional connection was equally as important as being physical.

“Obviously we didn’t talk for two years, and we were kind of like right back at it,” he said. “That is a real thing, and I fell short in those moments. I will acknowledge that. But there are numerous times where I tried to make steps to not allow us to get to a romantic place or a physical place and keep it as friends, because I wanted to be your friend.”
What Amanda Said at Part 1 of the ‘Summer House’ Reunion
When the reunion began, Amanda said she was prepared to “take accountability and apologize, especially to Ciara and Kyle.”
To that, Ciara replied, “I’m glad you could say our name this time” — a reference to the fact that Amanda and West’s joint statement did not address them specifically.
When Mia accused Amanda of prioritizing her relationship with West over her friendship with Ciara, Amanda defended herself.
“I had an awakening. I was living on my own. I had separated from my ex. I was trying to prioritize myself,” she said.
“I was also hanging out with other people. I was not just hanging out with West, OK? I was plenty of other friends where he was not present at all.”

The heart of the discussion was the extent of Amanda and West’s betrayal. When Andy asked the group to weigh in on Amanda and West’s joint statement revealing their relationship, West said there were “a lot of layers to it.”
“Is that enough layers to go, ‘What the f--- are we doing?’” cast member Carl Radke said.

“Right, it was so conscious that you guys talked about it,” Ciara said. “It wasn’t like a drunk hookup. You guys talked about it. You knew how dumb and how weird it would be, and you still did it. You chose to do it. You kept doing it. And you were hiding it.”
Amanda said, “Sometimes you can’t help who you like and who you are attracted to.”
“You actually can help it,” Ciara said. “When you’re married you can help it.”
Later, Amanda said what she “should have said” to Ciara when rumors about her and West first circulated online.
“Maybe I should have said from the get-go, I don’t know what’s going on, I’m having feelings for someone that I shouldn’t have feelings for, I don’t know what to do about it,” Amanda said.
“Then you remove yourself,” Ciara said.
“So I’m not allowed to pursue something that I feel strongly about?” Amanda replied.
“Well clearly you don’t feel strongly if you’re trying to understand it,” Kyle said.
“So no one in this world has ever been in a situation like this before?” Amanda asked.
“In high school maybe,” Lindsay said. “We’re in our 30s!”
“This is not the craziest thing that’s ever happened in the whole entire world,” Amanda said. “You guys are acting like I had an affair.”
Ciara and West kissed the last weekend of the summer, shown in finale episode of Season 10.
“Were you aware they were canoodling?” Andy asked Amanda.
“Not to that extent, no,” she said.

title: Joe Jonas Recalls ‘Bad Audition’ for This Popular Apple TV Show: ‘I Crash and Burn’
summary: Joe Jonas recalls his “bad audition” for the popular Apple TV show “The Morning Show.”
story: Joe Jonas is cringing over his crummy audition for the hit Apple TV series “The Morning Show.”

The Jonas Brothers singer recalled how he bombed his audition for the series, which stars Jennifer Aniston and Reese Witherspoon, while chatting with brothers Nick Jonas and Kevin Jonas on the May 26 episode of their “Hey Jonas!” podcast.
“I show up early and they’re like, ‘Oh.’ And they did this thing which was very kind, they were like, ‘Oh, we have a private room for you,’” Joe Jonas recalled.
The 36-year-old musician quickly realized he had been placed in an area where he could easily listen as other actors auditioned for the same role.
“This is the saddest room you can imagine,” he joked. “I could hear clear as day all the guys auditioning and these guys were crushing it.

“And I go in and I just crash and burn,” he said, not sharing the role he went out for. “... It was literally like, I don’t even know what I’m auditioning for... It was just gone for me. And it was such a bad audition.”
“Bad auditions happen,” Nick Jonas told his older brother before asking, “Would you say that’s your worst audition?”
“I think also ‘Wicked’ was a bit of a strange one,” Joe Jonas responded, “because there was, like, incredible theater vocalists I could hear clear as day.”
While speaking to Entertainment Weekly in 2025, the brothers teased Joe Jonas over his “terrible” audition for his starring role in the 2008 Disney movie musical “Camp Rock.”

“Joe got the role of Shane Gray in ‘Camp Rock’ as our band was already doing its thing for a couple years,” Nick Jonas recalled. “Joe gets this call to audition for this Disney movie, and I recall us helping him do his self-tape audition in our hotel room. Like a Holiday Inn in Albuquerque or something.”
“I wish we could find this footage,” Kevin Jonas interjected.
“Disney has it somewhere,” Joe Jonas responded. “In their vaults.”
The brothers have also competed against each other for roles.
Nick Jonas previously confirmed to Variety that both he and Joe Jonas went up for the role of Fiyero in “Wicked.”
“Joe and I both went out and we didn’t get it. But that’s the life of an actor,” he said of the part, which went to actor Jonathan Bailey.
In 2023, Joe Jonas recalled how Nick Jonas crushed his dreams when he was chosen over him for a coach role on “The Voice.”

“I remember — and I don’t even know if you know this, some more tea that hasn’t been spilled — it was at the most picturesque setting, it was at the Fleetwood Mac show at MSG (Madison Square Garden), and as they’re playing ‘Landslide,’ I found out that he was going to be a judge on ‘The Voice,’” Joe Jonas revealed on the May 22, 2023 episode of Dax Shepard’s “Armchair Expert” podcast.
“I was so jealous. I cried my eyes out to ‘Landslide,’” he joked.
“It was bittersweet, of course, because I’m super happy for him,” added the singer, who went on to be a judge on Season 7 of the Australian version of the show.
“But I’m also bummed because I want that f------ gig.”

title: BTS Is Releasing Limited-Edition Oreos: What’s the Flavor?
summary: Oreo and BTS announced they had joined forces (and their considerable fanbases) to launch the Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies in a hotteok-inspired flavor.
story: When BTS sang, “One day it’ll be a good day, for sure,” they might have meant today.
On May 28, Oreo and BTS announced they had joined forces (and their considerable fanbases) to launch the Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies.
Meant to emulate hotteok, the popular Korean street food, the brown sugar pancake-flavored Oreo cookie was curated by the seven-member group and dedicated to its fans.

The Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies are filled with a sweet creme which remixes the flavors of hotteok, a fried stuffed pancake made with yeasted dough and typically filled with brown sugar, cinnamon and nuts.

“We took inspiration from it and explained to OREO what that flavor tastes like,” Jin said in a press release. He also called hotteok a “national snack” and said the cookie “really” tastes a lot like it. “We wanted to share with everyone the kinds of dessert flavors Koreans enjoy.”
“Yeah, it really has that flavor, so it’s fascinating,” J-Hope added in the release.
BTS said the whole band has “fond childhood memories” of eating both hotteok and Oreo cookies, so combining them seemed like a perfect fit.
RM added that they “ate quite a lot (of OREOs) during this song camp.”

“For OREO to be the first snacking brand we’ve collaborated with globally is a huge honor,” said the band, which just won Artist of the Year at the American Music Awards (AMAs) for the second time in a row. “We’re just so proud to add our own chapter to OREO’s amazing story.”
In honor of the band’s 13th anniversary, the cookies feature 13 unique embossments, including band member names, a BTS light stick and three Oreo cookies which form a special message to BTS fans.
Once BTS Army members collect all three, they’ll unveil the message. (Note that unique embossments included within each pack will vary.)
“Of course the taste matters, but it’s also really pretty,” Jimin said. “They made it really well.”

Oreo is also attempting to help create the world’s largest love letter to BTS, inspired by the fandom’s letter-writing tradition. Beginning on June 8, fans can scan a QR code on the BTS Oros or visit us.OREOBTS.com to write a playful digital letter to BTS.
Letter-writers can also continue to submit letters for a chance to win exclusive Oreo brand & BTS prizes.

The Limited Edition Oreo & BTS Cookies will be available for presale starting on June 1, at OREO.com/UnwrapTheCollab. The cookies will begin rolling out at retailers beginning June 8 and be available for a limited time, while supplies last.
Believe it: This news will light the cookie world up like dynamite.

title: Are ‘Summer House’ Stars West Wilson and Amanda Batula Still Together?
summary: Are “Summer House” stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson still together now? Here’s their relationship status ahead of the reunion.
story: “Summer House” stars Amanda Batula and West Wilson will make their debut as a couple in the first installment of the Bravo series’ three-part Season 10 reunion on May 26.

Just one day before part one aired, Wilson spoke about the status of his relationship with Batula on his podcast, confirming they are “doing our best” while navigating going out as a couple in public.
Batula and Wilson sparked controversy when they confirmed their secret romance in a joint statement March 31.
The confirmation came three months after Batula’s split from co-star and husband Kyle Cooke in January.
The new romance also caused a rift between Batula and her close friend and co-star Ciara Miller, who had an on-and-off romance with Wilson for years.
A trailer for the Season 10 reunion released May 19 shows Miller, Cooke and other castmates sounding off about Batula and Wilson’s relationship.
In one tense moment, Miller tells Batula she had been her “champion” for six years.
“I couldn’t fathom that I would be sitting here pissed that you’re f------ my ex,” she adds.
Meanwhile, Wilson is seen defending Batula and himself when host Andy Cohen asks if the pair were “in love.”
“This isn’t like a sex scandal,” replies Wilson, adding that pair grew close while “hanging out.”
Here’s what to know about Batula and Wilson’s controversial love story.
March 31: Batula and Wilson Confirmed Their Secret Romance
After weeks of online speculation, Batula and Wilson confirmed they were dating in a joint statement March 31 in their Instagram Stories.
The pair explained they had formed a “connection” out of a “genuine, longstanding friendship.”

“We’ve shown up for each other as friends over the years, through all the highs and lows, and what’s developed recently was the last thing either of us expected,” they wrote in their statement, which came three months after Batula’s split from co-star and husband Cooke in January.

“As our feelings evolved, we wanted to take time to understand exactly what we were feeling,” they added. “We also recognize that this has had an impact beyond just us and never wanted our actions to cause any hurt or be perceived as careless.”
Batula and Wilson said it was never their intention “to purposely hide anything.”
“Given the complicated relationship dynamics involved and the scrutiny that comes with being on a reality show, we needed a little space to process things privately before speaking on it,” they said.
April 1: Cooke Says He’s ‘Really Worried’ About Ex-Wife Batula
A day after Batula and Wilson announced their romance, Batula’s ex Cooke revealed on the “So Bad It’s Good With Ryan Bailey” podcast, that he was “really worried” about his ex-wife.
“I understand people have all sorts of opinions, and I’m not justifying any behavior. But like, from what I’m seeing, she’s kind of getting cyber bullied,” Cooke said.
Cooke added that he had concerns, too, about Wilson, who “seems to be the kind of guy playing multiple women at the same time.”
April 10: Batula Apologizes To ‘Everyone’ She’s ‘Disappointed and Hurt’
Less than two weeks after Batula and Wilson confirmed their union, Batula issued an apology to “everyone” she’s “disappointed and hurt.”

“Hi guys, I’ll keep this short and sweet,” she wrote in her Instagram Stories. “I’ve obviously been MIA, but I wanted to come back on and say that I’m truly sorry to everyone I’ve disappointed and hurt, especially those I know personally who I’ve reached out to individually.”

“For the sake of my mental health,” she continued, “I’m going to try to start living life with some sense of normalcy. If you see me out or posting online, please know that this still weighs very heavily on me. I’m not ignoring what’s happened or what’s unfolded.”
April 17: Miller Breaks Her Silence About the Romance
Miller spoke publicly for the first time about how she felt after learning her close friend Batula was dating her ex-boyfriend Wilson in a candid interview with Glamour published April 17.
Miller said she learned of the romance only 24 hours before Batula and Wilson released their statement and called the shock a “major mindf---.”
Miller explained she and Wilson had been “working on (their) relationship” last year and that Wilson attended her 30th birthday party in December 2025.
Miller also told the publication she felt more betrayed by Batula than Wilson, explaining, “At the end of the day, a guy’s a guy.”
“Whether or not West and I are working on a relationship, you just can’t put anything past a man,” she said.
“But I just never would think that it would come from someone like Amanda, who has been what has felt like in my circle and in my corner for so long. I think that’s the craziest part,” Miller added.
April 18: Batula and Wilson Attend a Yankees Game Together
Batula and Wilson made their first public appearance as a couple when they attended a New York Yankees baseball game on April 18.
The Yes Network posted a video on Instagram that showed the couple kissing in the stands of Yankee Stadium.
April 20: Wilson Says He and Batula Got ‘Serious’ in February
Wilson addressed the controversy around his romance with Batula on the April 20 episode of his podcast, “Show Me Something.”
Wilson told listeners there was “no overlap” between his relationship with Batula and either of their past relationships with Miller and Cooke.
“I know there’s a thousand different theories on the internet right now, but that is one thing that for sure did not happen,” said Wilson.

“Everyone was single, like I said earlier,” he added. “So then we realized things are maybe a bit serious in February, and that’s when this all started, and there’s not a whole lot more to it than that.”
April 23: The Cast Films the Season 10 Reunion
Wilson and Batula reunited with Miller and Cooke and the rest of their “Summer House” cast when they showed up to film the show’s Season 10 reunion in New York City on April 23.
May 5: Wilson Says He’s ‘Caused A Lot of Damage’
On the May 5 episode of his podcast, Wilson expressed hope that the tensions among the show’s cast members would ease.
He also said he regretted how much pain his romance with Batula had caused.

“These are my friends, and I’ve caused a lot of damage, which f---ing sucks. So it’s going to take time, for sure, but time helps,” he said. “But doing the reunion was one step, and got through it, and so we’re on the other side of it. So we’re just going to take it one day at a time.”
May 15: Cooke Says His Split with Batula Is ‘Amicable’
Cooke opened up about his relationships with both Batula and Wilson during a mid-May appearance on Jason Tartick’s “Trading Secrets” podcast.
Cooke said it was important for him to keep his breakup with Batula “amicable.” He added that he had “so much love” for his ex.
As for Wilson, Cooke said the two men had been in touch the previous week.

“I’m not the kind of guy that holds grudges, so obviously this is a very unfortunate situation. I don’t think people realize how close we were,” Cooke said. “So it’s tough. This is uncharted territory for me.”
May 25: Wilson Confirms He and Batula Are Still Together
Wilson confirmed he and Batula are still together on the May 25 episode of his podcast “Show Me Something.”
Asked by co-host Sophie Cunningham if he and Batula are “good,” he said they are “doing our best.”
“Going out in public is kinda scary,” he said.
He also said he regrets initially lying about his relationship with Batula.
“Because now it’s like, no matter what happens or what I say I have not a lot of ground to stand on,” he said.

title: Paul Anthony Kelly Almost Quit Acting After 13 Years. His Wife’s 4 Words Changed His Mind
summary: “Love Story” star Paul Anthony Kelly explains how his wife told him not to quit acting before he landed the role of JFK Jr. on the FX series.
story: Paul Anthony Kelly almost took the off-ramp on his road to fame.

The actor, who played JFK Jr. in this year’s smash hit “Love Story: John F. Kennedy Jr. & Carolyn Bessette” says he had been auditioning for parts for 13 years before landing the plum role in the FX series and was almost ready to give up on acting career.
But his wife, Syd Widziszewski-Kelly, made sure he stayed the course.

“I remember sitting down with her, and we were just talking and I was like, ‘You know, I don’t think this is really working. Haven’t hit it yet.’ Thirteen years is a long time,” he said May 26 on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle.

“We were thinking about starting a family, so it was like — what weighs more than the other? But she was like, ‘No, just stick it out,’ like, ‘Give it another year.’ And then a couple weeks later, I get this phone call for this, and here we are.”
Kelly said he believes coming to terms with the possibility of not making it as an actor was the moment it all came together for him.
“I think that that’s exactly what happens,” he said.
“Sometimes, when you let go …” Sheinelle Jones began.
“Yup, the universe comes back and says, ‘Oh, let me get this for you,’” Kelly said.
Sheinelle and Kelly were both at this year’s Met Gala, which prompted Sheinelle to note that he seemed “grateful” to be there.
“I mean, it’s exciting. It’s exhilarating, you know. I was there with people who are now considered my peers, people I’ve looked up to forever,” he said.
As it turns out, Kelly was able to have the career and a family. “Love Story” premiered in February, the same month he and Widziszewski-Kelly welcomed a baby.

“You just kind of got to go with it,” he said. “The train’s going, you’re not stopping. Yeah, it was a really interesting experience to become a father, and then you know, like a month later, have the show come out and give birth again to a career.”

title: Who Is Kyle Busch’s Wife? What to Know About Samantha Busch
summary: Who is Kyle Busch’s wife, Samantha Busch? The two share two children. He died at 41 following a sudden illness.
story: Kyle Busch’s family is mourning the loss of the NASCAR champion following his death on May 21.
The Busch family has not publicly confirmed Busch’s cause of death, but they announced the 41-year-old’s passing in a joint statement with NASCAR and Richard Childress Racing.

“Our entire NASCAR family is heartbroken by the loss of Kyle Busch,” the statement read. “A future Hall of Famer, Kyle was a rare talent, one who comes along once in a generation. He was fierce, he was passionate, he was immensely skilled and he cared deeply about the sport and fans.”

The statement continued: “During this incredibly difficult time, we ask everyone to respect the family’s privacy and continue to keep them in your thoughts and prayers. Further updates will be shared as appropriate.”
The NASCAR driver leaves behind his wife, Samantha Busch, and two children: a son named Brexton and a daughter named Lennix.
In the wake of Kyle Busch’s death, learn more about the love of his life below.
Samantha Busch Has Her Own Podcast

Samantha Busch is the host of a podcast called “Certified Oversharer.” In March 2026, she welcomed her husband to the podcast and discussed a wide array of topics, ranging from their children to their religious faith.
During the discussion, Samantha Busch noted that she and her husband prayed together “every night” and were “getting deeper into our faith.”
The mother of two, who grew up Catholic, said she “never understood much of anything” about her religion.

“At Christian church, I feel very much more at home because you get to sing and I feel like the message, it’s just so much more casual and I’m able to understand that (and apply it) a lot better,” she said.
Samantha Busch Is a Graduate of Purdue University

Per her website, Samantha Busch spent her childhood in  northwestern Indiana. She went on to study psychology at Purdue University in her home state. She later got a master’s degree in industrial/organizational psychology.
Samantha Busch Met Kyle in 2007, and She Didn’t Know Who He Was
In 2023, Samantha Busch took to Instagram to share the story of how she first met her husband in 2007 in Indianapolis.
At the time, Samantha Busch was working at a promotional model agency and she was sent on assignment “to work for Chevy getting people hyped up for the races,” she wrote.
In her Instagram post, she recalled how a woman at the race track asked if she wanted to go for a ride around the track. She said she accepted since it was “crazy hot out.”
As it turns out, Kyle Busch had seen his future wife “from afar” and had asked this woman to speak to her.

“So needless to say I hopped in the car and the first thing I said when I saw him in his Chevy tee and ice cold car was ‘wow you got a way better assignment then I did.’ He looked at me funny assuming I knew who he was and we were off. I did most of the talking, big surprise, and then invited him to hang out with a group of us going out that night to which he declined bc he had to work. Little did I know he had a truck race,” she wrote.
Samantha Busch still didn’t know who he was when they started texting, but a friend’s father figured it out.
“Then I got super nervous so we talked on the phone for 4 months before seeing each other and going on a date and the rest is history!” she recalled.
Samantha Busch Married Kyle Busch in 2010
Three years after they first met, the couple got married in 2010. Their wedding was filmed for a Style Network special called the “Fast and the Fabulous: A NASCAR Wedding,” per The Los Angeles Times.
Samantha Busch Is a Mother of Two
Over the course of their 16-year marriage, Kyle and Samantha Busch welcomed two children together. Their son, Brexton, arrived in 2015, followed by their daughter, Lennix, who arrived in 2022.
The couple appeared on TODAY in 2025 to discuss their journey with infertility and revealed that they experienced multiple miscarriages on their path to becoming parents of two children.
The couple underwent in vitro fertilization (IVF) to conceive their son and welcomed their daughter with the assistance of a gestational carrier.
Samantha Bush Wrote a Book About Her Experience with Infertility
In 2021, Samantha Bush published a book called “Fighting Infertility: Finding My Inner Warrior Through Trying to Conceive, IVF, and Miscarriage.”
During her TODAY appearance in 2025, Samantha Busch spoke about the experience of navigating infertility, calling it “very isolating.”
“For over 10 years of trying to have our family, social media was wonderful. They were with us through every transfer, every miscarriage, every trial and cheered us on along the way,” she said.

After their experiences with infertility, Kyle and Samantha Busch created the Bundle of Joy Fund to raise awareness about infertility and spearhead fundraising efforts for couples experiencing infertility.
She Was on a Reality Show
In 2019, Samantha Busch appeared in the CMT reality series, “Racing Wives,” which aired for one season.
The show featured five wives of professional racers, including Samantha Busch, Amber Balcaen, Whitney Ward Dillon, Ashley Busch (the now ex-wife of Kyle Busch’s brother, Kurt Busch) and Mariel Lane.

title: Is Jack Antonoff Attending Taylor Swift’s Wedding? How He Answered a Question About ‘Big Weddings this Summer’
summary: Jack Antonoff dodged a question about attending Taylor Swift’s wedding to Travis Kelce when he appeared on TODAY. Here’s how he answered.
story: If Jack Antonoff knows anything about when Taylor Swift’s wedding to Travis Kelce is taking place, he’s not saying.

Antonoff and his band, Bleachers, rocked TODAY on May 22. Antonoff, of course, is a 13-time Grammy Award winner and close confidante of Swift, having written dozens of songs with her, so Craig Melvin used his appearance on TODAY to try to find out some information about the highly anticipated nuptials.
“Wedding season is upon us,” Craig Melvin said.
“Is it?” Antonoff replied.
The pair then joked a bit about Craig’s marriage to Lindsay Czarniak before Craig vaguely turned the matter around.
“Are you going to any big weddings this summer?” he said, not mentioning Swift and Kelce by name.
“I hope. Yeah, I hope so, man,” he said before making a couple of silly noises. “I love being married. I’m happy you love it too.”
“That’s all you’re gonna give me, huh?” Craig laughed playfully asked as Antonoff, who’s married to actor Margaret Qualley, stared at him.
“Did we just have a moment?” Antonoff asked as Craig continued to howl.
“I think we did. I think we did have a moment,” Craig replied. “That’s a little awkward.”

Swift and Kelce announced their engagement in August 2025. Details about the event have been kept under wraps. Swift did attend Antonoff’s wedding in 2023, before she and the Kansas City Chiefs tight end were known to be an item.
It is unclear when Swift and Kelce will tie the knot.
“Do we think there’s a wedding this year?” Savannah Guthrie asked Kylie Kelce, who’s married to Travis Kelce’s brother, Jason Kelce, back in January on TODAY.
“Oh, I don’t know,” Kelce replied with a shrug.
“OK. Do you really not know?” Savannah followed up.
“I have no idea,” Kelce said before smiling and laughing.

title: Sheinelle Jones Gets a Major Compliment While Dancing With Legendary Choreographer Fatima Robinson
summary: Sheinelle Jones is channeling Beyoncé, Prince and more legendary artists as she steps onto the dance floor with acclaimed choreographer Fatima Robinson.
story: Sheinelle Jones is channeling Beyoncé, Prince and more legendary artists as she steps onto the dance floor with acclaimed choreographer Fatima Robinson.

Robinson, a “pioneer” of hip-hop dance who helped bring the genre into mainstream pop culture, appeared on TODAY with Jenna & Sheinelle Friday, May 22, and reflected on her decades-long career working with some of the biggest icons in music.

The choreographer, director and creative producer was just 21 years old when she collaborated with Michael Jackson in the early ‘90s for his unforgettable “Remember the Time” music video. But she tells Sheinelle Jones that her love for dance started long before then.
“I would record music videos and learn the steps. That was my classroom, you know?” she explained. At the time, her mother was against her pursuing a career in dance.

“Parents didn’t really understand art in that way. They were taught that art can’t support you. Whereas I was like, ‘No, this is where I feel closest to God, so I have to follow this feeling,’” she remembered thinking as a child. Her mother was religious, so Robinson decided to make dancing her form of church.
Sheinelle stepped into Robinson’s world and asked the “queen of dance” for a few tips. The TODAY co-host showed off her moves as the two danced around a rehearsal studio.
“You can dance,” Robinson said to a delighted Sheinelle. “No, like you can dance dance.”
Robinson began as a self-taught club dancer in the early days of the Los Angeles hip-hop scene. But even after collaborating with the King of Pop, she still faced self-doubt.

“Of course I had impostor syndrome. I mean, I got thrown into it,” she recalled about the first stage of her career. “I would also question why I was there. You know, you definitely have those moments. But you push through those, because you are supposed to be there. And you know what? You’re not going to have the answer to every question. You answer it, and it’s OK to not know. And it’s also OK to change your answer.”
She also encountered critics in the beginning who doubted the success of hip-hop.

“I bumped up against people really thinking hip-hop dance was a true art form of dance. They saw it as a street dance, it was a passing fad. And so for me in the beginning of my career, that was part of my goal, is for people to see us as true dancers,” Robinson explained.
She continued, “Look at it now. Hip-hop is being taught everywhere. It’s all over YouTube and TikTok. I like to say, I’m one of the pioneers of that dance.”
When she sees viral TikTok dances, she appreciates how far the genre has come.

Since her breakout moment with Jackson, Robinson has worked with Aaliyah, Pharrell Williams, Mary J. Blige, Backstreet Boys, Rihanna and more artists. She described Prince, whom she partnered with as well, as “one of one.”
Robinson has also become Beyoncé’s long-time collaborator, with the two teaming up from the Grammy winner’s “Dreamgirls” era to her “Renaissance Tour.”
“We work so hard. She is such a beast when it comes to work, and I love that work ethic, because it really inspires you to get in there, grind and make it happen,” Robinson said.

The choreographer has been nominated for three Emmys and was recently honored with the Arthur Mitchell Visionary Award from the Dance Theatre of Harlem. She accepted the award from friend and mentor Rosie Perez.
After decades of choreography, Robinson told Sheinelle that the art form still makes her feel alive.
“You feel connected to the universe,” she said about the power of dance. “There is a way in which you move the energy in your body, it makes sense. You’re closer to the intuition that you hold.”
She added, “Dancers will know, it is your true being and your soul coming together as one. It’s really beautiful.”

title: Spotify to Reserve Concert Tickets for Artists’ Biggest Fans. How It’ll Work
summary: Spotify is hoping to make the concert ticket purchasing experience less stressful for artists’ most dedicated listeners.
story: Spotify is ushering out a new perk for superfans.

The streaming service announced on May 21 their new Reserved experience for Spotify premium subscribers. The new program hopes to make the concert ticket buying experience less stressful by holding two tour tickets for artists’ top listeners.

“No racing against thousands of other fans, no hunting for codes, just a reserved window to purchase before tickets are on sale to the general public,” Spotify said in a press release. “You show up for the artist, Spotify shows up for you.”
So, how will this work and what do Spotify users need to do? Read more below.
What Is Reserved on Spotify?
With Reserved, Spotify premium users, 18 years and older in the U.S., the streaming service will reserve two concert tickets for artists’ biggest fans.
The streaming platform will identify the singers’ top listeners, making sure “they are real humans and not bots,” and give them the opportunity to buy two tickets during a specific time.
How Will Reserved on Spotify Work?
Reserved kicks off this summer when select artists will have the opportunity to dedicate tickets to Spotify’s new program.
Ticket availability will vary by artist, tour and location, Spotify states. Eligibility is based on the user’s engagement with their favorite artist or artists on Spotify.
First, users will need to make sure their preferred location is enabled in the Live Events Feed and that notifications are turned on for the latest news.

Those eligible for tickets will receive an email and an in-app notification.Up to two concert tickets on the tour will be available for a user to purchase during a dedicated timeframe (about a day). The purchase of the ticket will be completed on the ticketing partner’s platform during that window.
Those eligible for tickets will receive an email and an in-app notification.

Up to two concert tickets on the tour will be available for a user to purchase during a dedicated timeframe (about a day). The purchase of the ticket will be completed on the ticketing partner’s platform during that window.

It should be noted that if a tour is not coming to the users’ preferred location, they will not receive an offer. If a user does receive an offer, they will be able to pick and choose their preferred date, location and seats at checkout.
The experience is built around real fan engagement and those who listen most to an artist will be rewarded with the opportunity to purchase tickets ahead of others.

title: Stephen Colbert Spoke Straight to the Audience in His Final ‘Late Show’ Episode. What He Said
summary: Listen to the last message Stephen Colbert delivered to the studio audience. Here’s exactly what he said.
story: Before he got to all of the star-studded guests who stopped by, Stephen Colbert shared a moment of gratitude with the audience as he kicked off the final episode of “The Late Show” on May 21.
Colbert delivered a brief address to his studio audience and viewers at home before his monologue, letting them know what doing the show, which he had hosted since 2015, has meant.

“Folks, we have done over 1,800 of these shows. And most nights I come out here and I talk to the audience beforehand. And tonight I thought I’d talk to the audience in here and the audience out there at home,” he began.

“This show, I want you to know, and you to know, has been a joy for us to do for you. In fact, we call this show ‘The Joy Machine,’ alright?” he said looking over at bandleader Louis Cato, who led the show’s house band, The Great Big Joy Machine.
“We call it the Joy Machine because to do this many shows, it has to be a machine,” Colbert continued.

“But the thing is, if you choose to do it with joy, it doesn’t hurt as much when your fingers get caught in the gears. And I cannot adequately explain to you what the people who work here have done for each other and how much we mean to each other, so I would just say to them you are all the great Achilles whom we knew,” he said as the audience cheered.
Colbert, who previously hosted the satirical news show “The Colbert Report,” from 2005 to 2014, then discussed what he learned hosting “The Late Show.”

“Now, on night one of ‘The Colbert Report,’ back in the day, I said anyone can read the news to you. I promised to feel the news at you. And I realized pretty soon in this job that our job over here was different,” he said. “We were here to feel the news with you, and I don’t know about you, but I sure have felt it.

“And I just want to let all y’all know, in here and out there, how important you’ve been to what we have done. The energy that you have given us, we sincerely need that to have done the best possible show we could’ve for you for the last 11 years. You’ve given to us, we’ve given it all right back to you. What would you call that, Louis?”
Cato then replied with a heartfelt take on the matter.
“I would call that a reciprocal emotional relationship,” he said.
“That’s exactly right, because we love doing the show for you, but we really, really love is doing the show with you,” Colbert responded.
“Now, I’ll say to you what I’ve said to every audience for the last 11 years, and I have meant it every time. Have a good show. Thanks for being here, and let’s do it, y’all.”
Colbert, who revealed in July 2025 that “The Late Show” was being canceled, served as host since 2015, after David Letterman, who had hosted the program since it launched in 1993, retired.
The time slot will be replaced by “Comics Unleashed” hosted by Byron Allen.

title: Jewelers Break Down Lainey Wilson’s Eye-Catching and Unique Engagement Ring Worth Up to $250,000
summary: Lainey Wilson is officially a married woman. What is her engagement ring like? How much is it worth?
story: Lainey Wilson is officially a married woman.
On May 10, the “Heart Like a Truck” singer tied the knot with her longtime love, Devlin “Duck” Hodges, a retired NFL quarterback who now works as an entrepreneur.
Per Vogue, the couple said “I do” at the foot of a waterfall on a cobblestone ledge in Dickson, Tennessee.
Shortly after their nuptials, the newlyweds attended the 2026 Academy of Country Music Awards in Las Vegas on May 17 and all eyes were on Wilson’s ring finger.
The bride’s diamond wedding band was certainly a novelty after she made her marriage official, but her intricate engagement ring was the true talker.
To learn more about the unique ring, TODAY.com sought the expertise of several jewelry pros. Below, they’re sharing all the gorgeous details about the massive sparkler.
Wilson Has a Contemporary Cluster Engagement Ring
Wilson’s engagement ring has a lot of intricate details.
Anubh Shah, the co-founder of engagement ring brand With Clarity, says it’s best described as a “diamond cluster ring with a cocktail ring aesthetic.”

“Instead of featuring a single center stone, the design combines multiple diamond shapes — including oval, pear, marquise and round stones — arranged in a sculptural, floral-inspired cluster that feels bold and dramatic,” Shah says.
Patricia Curts, the co-founder and managing director of UK-based jewelry brand The Mexican Collection, notes that three large diamonds “anchor” the center of the ring.

“An elongated cushion cut sits on the left, an oval on the right and a downward-facing pear at the bottom. Smaller pavé diamonds extend outward around them, creating a floral effect that gives the whole piece a sense of movement,” Curts says.
The Ring Style Was Once Considered ‘The Height of Romance’
Cluster rings “peaked in popularity during the Victorian and Edwardian periods,” per Curts.
Chris Bajda, an e-commerce entrepreneur and the managing partner of Grooms Day, says cluster rings, not solitaire rings that dominate now, were once “considered the height of romance.”
“The solitaire became the standard not because it was always the tradition but because it was heavily marketed as one,” he says.
Curts says the current resurgence of cluster rings is due to consumers’ “desire for something more personal rather than conventional.”
“This was already starting to happen before Lainey’s ring, but a beautifully crafted custom piece like this is making it happen at an even faster pace than before,” Curts says.
The Ring May Be Worth Up to $250,000
Determining the value of a custom piece of jewelry is “quite challenging,” per Curts.
“This one has a wide range depending on factors you simply can’t determine from photos alone: Diamond clarity, color grade and cut quality all move the needle considerably,” she says.
However, Curts estimates that the ring costs between $50,000 and 250,000 or above since the three primary diamonds appear to be approximately 2.50 carats each.
Ellison thinks the ring has between 7 and 10 carats of diamonds and was custom made by hand, which could put it in the $50,000  to $250,000 range.
The Pros and Cons of Cluster Rings
The biggest advantage to cluster rings is that you get “a lot more visual impact for your money,” Bajda says.

“Multiple smaller stones grouped can look just as stunning as a single large diamond at a fraction of the cost. For couples who want a ring that turns heads without spending six figures, a cluster is a really smart choice,” he says.
Design flexibility is another advantage to cluster rings.

“You can go floral, starburst, geometric or vintage and the style options are almost endless. That kind of freedom to express personality through design is something a solitaire just cannot match in the same way,” Bajda says.
The downside to cluster rings is the maintendance they require compared to simpler settings. For example, individual stones can loose and fall out if the ring isn’t inspected regularly.
“With multiple stones and intricate metal work, the prong and setting need to be checked much more often,” Curts says.

title: Stephen Colbert Kept the Guests for His Last Episode a Secret. Who Showed Up?
summary: Who were the surprise guests on the last episode of Stephen Colbert’s last show? See the full list.
story: “The Late Show With Stephen Colbert” signed off with a little help from the host’s friends.
The final episode of the long-running late-night TV series aired May 21 and featured a star-studded collection of guests and cameos.
The series launched in 1993 with original host David Letterman. Colbert took over in 2015 after Letterman retired. CBS announced in July 2025 that it would be canceling the show.
Here’s a look at who appeared on the final episode:
Paul McCartney

The Beatles legend served as the final guest and gave Colbert a picture of the Beatles on “The Ed Sullivan Show”in 1964. That show, of course, was shot in Ed Sullivan Theater, which is where “The Late Show” taped.
Jon Batiste and Elvis Costello

McCartney also helped close the show after original bandleader Jon Batiste and Elvis Costello headlined as performance that ended with Colbert saying goodnight. Before they signed off, though, McCartney appeared onstage, as he, Colbert and the other acts sang the Beatles’ classic “Hello, Goodbye” to close out the episode.
Bryan Cranston

The Emmy-winning “Breaking Bad” star, sitting in the audience, appeared when he interrupted Colbert’s opening monologue, feigning disappointment when he was told there were too many guests to squeeze him in.
A crestfallen and hurt Cranston barged out of the theater and tossed his “Late Show” hat in the audience. “You can keep your stupid hat, I’m gonna go sell my ticket,” he said.
Paul Rudd
The “Ant-Man” star, who was also in the audience, was gung ho about reading a long poem, only to be told that he would also not be the final guest.
“Well, I guess it’s a little awkward that I brought you the traditional retirement gift,” he replied, referencing a half dozen bananas he had with him.
Tim Meadows
The former “Saturday Night Live” star and longtime friend of Colbert’s, sitting in front of Rudd, was more open with his faux anger at being told he wouldn’t be the last guest.
“Screw you, Colbert!” he said before storming out with Rudd’s bananas.
Tig Notaro
